Cybersecurity in the TMT sector. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 20/08/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Amad Khan](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/amad-khan/index.html). IT, media and telecom companies are essential for almost every organisation. Their software and infrastructure are the gateway to a broad audience. That central role in our digital landscape makes them vulnerable. Cybercriminals can cause major damage to them, their clients and society. This may be why there are more hacking and deception attempts at companies in TMT than in other sectors. Recent cyberattacks in the sector. In mid-2023 the notorious ransomware group LockBit [claimed](https://www.securityweek.com/tsmc-says-supplier-hacked-after-ransomware-group-claims-attack-on-chip-giant/) they had hacked chip giant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC).

text: According to the company itself, that was not correct. It said hardware supplier Kinmax had been hit, not TSMC.

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: According to the company itself, that was not correct. It said hardware supplier Kinmax had been hit, not TSMC. TSMC halted data exchanges with its supplier immediately after the incident.

text: In April 2023, hardware and software developer MSI reported it had fallen victim to a ransomware attack. [Private keys were stolen](https://www.ncsc.nl/actueel/nieuws/2023/mei/7/msi-ransomware-aanval), including Intel Boot Guard keys. These are used to digitally sign and verify motherboard firmware.

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: In April 2023, hardware and software developer MSI reported it had fallen victim to a ransomware attack. [Private keys were stolen](https://www.ncsc.nl/actueel/nieuws/2023/mei/7/msi-ransomware-aanval), including Intel Boot Guard keys. These are used to digitally sign and verify motherboard firmware. In theory, criminals could use the stolen keys to run malicious firmware and gain deep access to systems. Even so, the National Cyber Security Centre considers the chance of abuse small.

text: In April 2023, hardware and software developer MSI reported it had fallen victim to a ransomware attack. In theory, criminals could use the stolen keys to run malicious firmware and gain deep access to systems.

text: At the end of 2022, hackers [stole the personal data of tens of thousands of Delta Fiber customers](https://www.telegraaf.nl/financieel/843971726/grote-roof-persoonsgegevens-bij-delta-fiber).

metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: At the end of 2022, hackers [stole the personal data of tens of thousands of Delta Fiber customers](https://www.telegraaf.nl/financieel/843971726/grote-roof-persoonsgegevens-bij-delta-fiber).

text: Swift action kept the damage relatively limited.

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Swift action kept the damage relatively limited.

text: The company said it involved only names, addresses, email addresses, dates of birth, phone numbers and bank account numbers — no sensitive data such as passwords and credit card details. Even so, customers should be extra alert to possible email and phone fraud, such as [phishing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/phishing/index.html).

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The company said it involved only names, addresses, email addresses, dates of birth, phone numbers and bank account numbers — no sensitive data such as passwords and credit card details. Even so, customers should be extra alert to possible email and phone fraud, such as [phishing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/phishing/index.html). You can have your own cyber defences in good shape, yet still be embarrassed when hackers attack your supplier — TV providers like Ziggo and KPN have experienced this since March 2024. Hackers managed to [broadcast Russian propaganda videos on BabyTV](https://www.nu.nl/tech/6308299/tv-wereld-neemt-maatregelen-na-russische-propaganda-op-babytv.html). They used a jammer to break into Eutelsat’s satellite signal.

text: Because this type of attack had never happened before, no one was prepared, and it took a while before the hack was even noticed.

text: NIS2: new European legislation to improve cyber resilience. To improve cybersecurity in Europe, a new law will take effect in October 2024: NIS2, the successor to the Network and Information Security Directive (NIS).

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: NIS2: new European legislation to improve cyber resilience. To improve cybersecurity in Europe, a new law will take effect in October 2024: NIS2, the successor to the Network and Information Security Directive (NIS). Organisations that are important or essential to society must comply with this directive to limit the impact of cyberattacks and disruptions. This also applies to companies in the chain of these vital organisations, such as clients, partners and suppliers.

text: NIS2: new European legislation to improve cyber resilience. Organisations that are important or essential to society must comply with this directive to limit the impact of cyberattacks and disruptions.

This also applies to companies in the chain of these vital organisations, such as clients, partners and suppliers.

metadata.parent_text: NIS2: new European legislation to improve cyber resilience. To improve cybersecurity in Europe, a new law will take effect in October 2024: NIS2, the successor to the Network and Information Security Directive (NIS). Organisations that are important or essential to society must comply with this directive to limit the impact of cyberattacks and disruptions. This also applies to companies in the chain of these vital organisations, such as clients, partners and suppliers.

text: The intention is that European member states translate the directive into national legislation, but the Netherlands is running behind schedule. It is still wise to prepare your organisation for implementation now.

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The intention is that European member states translate the directive into national legislation, but the Netherlands is running behind schedule. It is still wise to prepare your organisation for implementation now. The law will come anyway, and clients or suppliers in other European countries will follow the directive from October 2024.

text:
Within TMT, several companies fall under NIS2, including:
Management of ICT services
Digital infrastructure
Digital service providers. Wondering whether your organisation falls under the NIS2 directive?

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity
metadata.author: Amad Khan
metadata.description: IT suppliers are a popular target for cybercriminals. Read more.
metadata.parent_text:
Within TMT, several companies fall under NIS2, including:
Management of ICT services
Digital infrastructure
Digital service providers. Wondering whether your organisation falls under the NIS2 directive? Read our report ‘Cyberattack does not always wake entrepreneurs up’, or complete the National Cyber Security Centre’s [NIS2 Self-assessment](https://www.ncsc.nl/actueel/nieuws/2023/oktober/18/zelf-evaluatie-nis2-gelanceerd). [Also read: Cyberattack does not always wake entrepreneurs up](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/cyberaanval-schudt-ondernemer-lang-niet-altijd-wakker.html)
More information. Read all the facts and developments in the report ‘Cybersecurity in the TMT sector’.
